observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to demand immediate settlement/ liquidation of unliquidated cash advances amounting to ₱11.533 million pursuant to existing regulations, otherwise, require the persons accountable to refund the cash advances granted to them.
observations.reason: The amount had increased in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting City Accountant initiate the filing of the request for authority to write off dormant accounts following the requirements under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: The amount had increased in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Acting City Accountant, after verification of the documents in their office, prepares the Fund Utilization Report for all implemented projects, prepares the representation to the Source Agency and prepares Journal Entry Vouchers to record entries in the books to correct balances in the Due to NGAs account.

We further recommended and the City Budget Officer agreed to coordinate with the City Planning and Development Officer regarding projects funded by the 20% DF- continuing appropriations, especially those that remained unobligated and, for projects no longer feasible, make representation to the City Mayor to realign the projects to other priority development projects.
observations.reason: Review by the concerned officials is still ongoing for prior years’ projects with funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Department Heads concerned agreed to: a) inventory all documents in their offices to determine valueless records due for disposal; b) prepare the request for disposal and, upon approval, submit the same to the National Archives of the Philippines (NAP) in accordance with prescribed guidelines.
observations.reason: Offices had taken note to maintain only documents that are in use. No request for disposal had been made as of the report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create and organize an IAS independent of the Accounting and Control Division in compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 4177, to address weaknesses in the internal control system thus, reliability of financial information and operational efficiency.
observations.reason: There is only an Internal Control Division under the Office of the Mayor that functions as such.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant initiate action for the liquidation of the fund transfer by sending demand letters to the concerned groups/individuals for liquidation or return of funds if not utilized according to the Memorandum of Agreement; and demanding the return of the fund if found not utilized or used not in accordance with the purpose.
observations.reason: Some funds transfers to NGOs/POs were still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor directs the conduct of a proper investigation on all Accountable Officers at the City Treasurer’s Office to determine all persons responsible and liable for the irregularities that occurred therein, submit a report thereon, and file appropriate charges, if necessary, as soon as possible.
observations.reason: Forwarded to Fraud Audit for special audit investigation; however, no investigation had been instigated by the LGU itself.

observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended and the General Services Officer and members of the
Inventory Committee agreed to assess items classified as non-moving
inventories to determine existence and usability as of the current date and
use the First In, First Out (FIFO) method in inventories that are still usable
to avoid further obsolescence, issue RIS for each issuance, summarize to an
SSMI, and submit to the Accounting Office.
1.9. Further, we recommended and the concerned Accounting Office personnel
agreed to coordinate with the GSO and prepare the necessary adjusting
entries to fairly present the amount of inventory items in the financial
statements.

2.10. We recommended and the City Accountant agreed, in coordination with the
City Engineer, to prepare accounting entries to transfer to the related asset
accounts the amount of ₱186.856 million to correct the CIP accounts for CY
2024. Henceforth, perform periodic monitoring and reconciliations on the
CIP accounts to immediately identify and address discrepancies.

3.10. We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to immediately record all
reconciling items in the year of incurrence to reflect the correct balance of
the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements as of year-end pursuant
to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

4.10. We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require the Inventory
Committee to complete the required physical count of inventory items and
immediately submit to the Auditor an updated report for CY 2024.
Henceforth, prepare and submit subsequent RPCIs not later than January
31 of each year in accordance with the existing regulation.
4.11. We further recommended and the City Accountant agreed, in coordination
with the General Services Officer, to prepare the adjusting entries and effect
them in the books to reconcile the balances of inventory accounts between
the Accounting and GSO.

5.7. We recommended and the LGU personnel in-charged with property
management agreed to continue its sound practices to reinforce the LGU’s
commitment to financial integrity and compliance with applicable
regulations.

We recommended and the Accountant and GSO agreed to reconcile their records for the Accountant to prepare the necessary entries to record the 128 parcels of land to reflect the reliable balance of the Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to immediately record all the reconciling items in the year of incurrence to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements as of year-end pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to effectively cause the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur more development and to achieve the desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the local constituents.

We recommended that the City Planning and Development Officer, LDRRMO, and Heads of Implementing Offices agreed to validate the projects and programs funded under the LDRRMF Continuing Appropriations and determine their status.

We recommended the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, through the LDRRM Council and other concerned City officials, agreed to review and consolidate all available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of the DRRM funds to improve the City's ability to prepare for and respond to the threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to fast-track the segregation of documents in their office and immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the 206 disbursement vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents.

We recommended and the concerned City officials agreed that a reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and GSO be undertaken pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to fairly present the PPE account in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to analyze the accounts payable aging two years or more to confirm the validity of the claims, and if determined to be without valid claims, revert to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to fast-track the segregation of documents in her Office to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 69 checks issued, totaling ₱3.046 million to the Office of the Auditor. Henceforth, she should ensure that all RCIs submitted for audit are supported with complete DVs within the reglementary period.

We recommended and the City Budget Officer agreed to thoroughly examine projects in the continuing appropriations at year-end, especially those that remained unobligated and, for projects no longer feasible, make representation to the City Mayor to revert the corresponding allocation to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for appropriation to programs and projects that conform with the guidelines set in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-01. We also recommended and the City Planning and Development Coordinator agreed to formulate integrated economic, social, physical, and other development plans and policies for consideration of the local government development council and monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects, and activities in accordance with the approved development plan of the City.

We recommended and the Management agreed that, through its prior LDRRM Council, the concerned City officials will review and consolidate all available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of available funds. We also recommended and the City Officials agreed that the LDRRM Officer ensure the timely utilization of the fund to improve the City’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.
The city will review and follow the prescribed rules and regulations on the use of vaccines.

We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to immediately send collection or demand letters to delinquent stallholders and inform them of the possible consequences if they continue to defy the demand.
observations.reason: Letters were sent to the stallholders. Collections were made on some stall owners only and stallholders are still being followed up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Accountant and GSO agreed to reconcile their records on the Land account and for the Accountant to prepare the necessary entries to record the 128 parcels of land to reflect the reliable balance of Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to immediately record all the reconciling items in the year of incurrence to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements as of year- end pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the City Treasurer continue with the measures already implemented and invoke more diligence from accountable officers as to ensure compliance with prescribed rules and regulations.

observations.reason: Forwarded to Fraud Audit for special audit investigation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances amounting to P5,288,606.85 pursuant to existing regulations, otherwise require the persons accountable to refund the cash advances granted to them.
We also recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to refrain from granting additional cash advances to employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances, and to strictly comply with the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the same.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant, General Services Officer and City Engineer agreed to reconcile records between their offices pursuant to Section 266, GAM, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to fairly present the PPE accounts in the financial statement.
Furthermore, we recommended and they also agreed to investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to analyze the accounts payable aging two years or more to confirm the validity of the claims, and if determined to be without valid claims revert to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to fast track the segregation of documents in her Office to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 69 checks issued totaling P3,046,484.70 to the Office of the Auditor.  Henceforth, she should ensure that all RCIs submitted for audit are supported with complete DVs within the reglementary period.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Personnel-In-charge in each concerned office monitor the status of implementation for each project; prepare the summary report including the determination of the remaining balances, and timely submit reports to the Acting City Accountant for proper recording in the books; and
The Acting City Accountant, upon verification of reports, prepare the utilization report and refund balances of all completed projects to the Source Agency pursuant to the existing regulations.

We recommended and the City Budget Officer agreed to thoroughly examine projects in the continuing appropriations at year-end, especially those that remained unobligated and, for projects no longer feasible, make representation to the City Mayor to revert the corresponding allocation to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for appropriation to programs and projects that conform with the guidelines set in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
We also recommended and the City Planning and Development Coordinator agreed to formulate integrated economic, social, physical and other development plans and policies for consideration of the local government development council and monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects and activities in accordance with the approved development plan of the City.

We recommended and the Management agreed that, through its LDRRM Council, the concerned City officials will review and consolidate all available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of available funds.
We also recommended and the City Officials also agreed that the LDRRM Officer ensures the timely utilization of the fund to improve the City’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary reversion of the outstanding balance of the BGCM in the accounting books to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to require the recipient frontliners to submit the essential documents as proofs that they were eligible for the payment of ADHP pursuant to the prescribed guidelines and conditions and to justify the validity, regularity and propriety of the payments.

We recommended and the City officials agreed that the Program Coordinator at the City Health Office furnish the Acting City Accountant with the approved detailed list of allocated vaccines received and utilized by the City, and to recognize in the books the donations in accordance with existing regulations.
We recommended and the City Health Officer agreed to exert efforts to promote vaccine confidence and encourage more of its populace to vaccinate in order to attain the herd immunity.

We recommended and concerned LGU officials agreed that reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and GSO be undertaken pursuant to Section 266, GAM, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.

Furthermore, Accounting Office and GSO investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.
observations.managementAction: GSO will reconcile their record with Accounting books.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. Balances are still on-going reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Acting City Accountant immediately records in the specific bank accounts all the reconciling items found in the bank reconciliation statements to reflect the correct balances at year-end so that accuracy and reliability of the Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account can be validated.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 reconciling item were adjusted in the books in January, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances amounting to P6,215,913.08 pursuant to existing regulations, otherwise require the persons accountable to refund the cash advances granted to them.

We also recommended and the Acting City Accountant also agreed not to grant additional cash advances to employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances, and to strictly comply with the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the same.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to Accountable Officers.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. Only partial settlements were made as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed that the City GSO shall file an application for disposal of the unserviceable properties as provided for by existing regulations and the Acting City Accountant reclassify the cost of the unserviceable items to Other Assets Account during the disposal process and upon disposal drop them from the book of accounts.
observations.managementAction: GSO had submitted requests for disposal of some PPE.
observations.reason: Cost of unserviceable properties are still in the books awaiting results of inspection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LGU agreed that, through its LDRRM Council, the concerned City officials will review and consolidate all available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of available funds.

We also recommended and the LGU also agreed that the LDRRM Officer ensures the timely utilization of the fund to improve the City’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. Usage for the CY 2021 was still minimal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed that the City Mayor thru the BAC Secretariat require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the suppliers, and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases.
observations.managementAction: Omnibus Sworn Statements (OSS) were required and presented for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Budget Officer agreed that he will thoroughly examine projects in the continuing appropriations, especially those that remained unobligated at year-end. Representation to the Local Chief Executive shall be made so that appropriations no longer needed will be recommended for immediate reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for appropriation to programs and projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: Appropriations were examined.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to fast track the segregation of documents in her Office and timely submit all DVs with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

Further, we recommended and the Acting City Accountant also agreed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 76 checks issued totaling P2,903,929.30 to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, Notices of Suspension for these disbursements will be issued.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required DVs.
observations.reason: Reiterated for this year’s report for transactions for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Department Heads concerned agreed to: inventory all documents in their offices to determine valueless records due for disposal; and prepare the request for disposal and upon approval submit the same to the National Archives of the Philippines (NAP) in accordance with prescribed guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Offices had taken note to maintain only documents that are in use.
observations.reason: No request for disposal had been made as of report date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the General Service Officer agreed to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to concerned offices not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.

We also recommended and the General Service Officer agreed to maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the ledger cards maintained in the Accounting Office.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Sanggunian Panlungsod furnish the City General Services Office and the Accounting Office of their supplies and materials issuances held for distribution in order to accurately reflect the correct amount of inventory of P83,200,340.42 in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: (a) The GSO informed that partial inventory for CY 2019 was made on motor vehicles and IT equipment and report was submitted.
observations.reason: Overtaken with new regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to immediately send collection or demand letters to 19 delinquent stallholders, and inform them of the possible consequences if they continue to defy the demand.

Though noted, we also recommended strict adherence to Revenue Code of the City and require legitimate stallholders to observe the terms and conditions of the contract, especially on the regular payment of rentals to continue enjoying the privileges and benefits attached thereto.
observations.managementAction: Collections were made on some stall owners
observations.reason: Not all of the fees had been accounted for and are still being followed up.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following: The BAC Office and the City Engineer’s Office as the end-user to meticulously plan the procurement activities, particularly the procurement of infrastructure projects taking into consideration the timetable for each process and provision for unforeseeable emergencies so as not to delay the implementation thereof. For those unimplemented projects, proceed with the procurement activities.

To the City Planning and Development Office to monitor the implementation of the projects to be able to complete the projects according to the specified contract duration.

To the City Engineers’ Office to fast track the implementation of infrastructure projects in order to achieve desirable outcomes for the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The CEO have already set the deadline for the submission of Program of Works and Plans to fast track the implementation of unutilized projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. The fund was not optimally utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create and organize an IAS independent of the City Accounting Division in compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 4177, to address weaknesses in the internal control system thus, reliability of financial information and operational efficiency is not compromised.

We recommended that the City Government of Kabankalan formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils (LDCs) taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.

We also recommended that the City strictly observe the guidelines on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management already cause the reversion of the unspent balance of the LDRRM Fund of more than five years to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and thereafter, appropriate it for other social services through legislative enactment.

We recommended also that the Local Chief Executive instruct the DRRM Officer to revise its LDRRMIP as well as to revise AIP to include PPAs for the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the special trust fund.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Mayor endorsed a resolution for the reversion of previous years’ LDRRMF.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, only a portion of the amount was reverted because of imminent charges from on-going projects verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects so that the optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained; and City Accountant and City Treasurer to immediately refund/remit the excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to avoid possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that the City Accountant initiate action for the liquidation of the outstanding fund transfer through sending demand letters to the NGOs/POs concerned and demanding return of funds, if not utilized or has been used not in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement; and further that the City Government follow strictly Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 on the granting of fund transfer to NGOs/POs.
observations.managementAction: Demand were made to the concerned groups/individuals for liquidation or return of funds, if not utilized according to purpose.
observations.reason: Some fund transfer to NGOs/POs were still outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant further analyze and evaluate unpaid obligations that remained outstanding for more than two years totaling P2,929,664.58 and effect its reversion to the unappropriated surplus, pursuant to Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

Likewise, we recommended that the City Accountant should also determine the reason for the P368,145.43 negative balance included in the account “Accounts Payable.” Adjusting entries should subsequently be prepared, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Evaluation of Accounts Payable is on-going.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to investigate the discrepancies and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to correct and reconcile the balances of Cash in Bank – Local Currency Time Deposits account with that of the bank balances, for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended and the General Service Officer agreed to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to concerned offices not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.
We also recommended and the General Service Officer agreed to maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the ledger cards maintained in the Accounting Office.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Sanggunian Panlungsod furnish the City General Services Office and the Accounting Office of their supplies and materials issuances held for distribution in order to accurately reflect the correct amount of inventory of P83,200,340.42 in the financial statements.

Though noted, we also recommended strict adherence to Revenue Code of the City and require legitimate stallholders to observe the terms and conditions of the contract, especially on the regular payment of rentals to continue enjoying the privileges and benefits attached thereto.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the processing and granting of applications for terminal leave benefits to its officials and employees.

We recommended the following:
The BAC Office and the City Engineer’s Office as the end -user to meticulously plan the procurement activities, particularly the procurement of infrastructure projects taking into consideration the timetable for each process and provision for unforeseeable emergencies so as not to delay the implementation thereof. For those unimplemented projects, proceed with the procurement activities.
To the City Planning and Development Office to monitor the implementation of the projects to be able to complete the projects according to the specified contract duration.
To the City Engineers’ Office to fast track the implementation of infrastructure projects in order to achieve desirable outcomes for the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to its constituents.

We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in the SEF budget programs for health, nutrition, early education and social services development for young children aged zero to four years to support the ECCD program of the government.

We recommended that Management create and organize an IAS independent of the City Accounting Division in compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 4177, to address weaknesses in the internal control system thus, reliability of financial information and operational efficiency is not compromised.

We recommended that the City Government of Kabankalan formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils (LDCs) taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.
We also recommended that the City strictly observe the guidelines on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that the City Government of Kabankalan formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils (LDCs) taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.
We also recommended that the City strictly observe the guidelines on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended and Management already cause the reversion of the unspent balance of the LDRRM Fund of more than five years to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and thereafter, appropriate it for other social services through legislative enactment.
We recommended also that the Local Chief Executive instruct the DRRM Officer to revise its LDRRMIP as well as to revise AIP to include PPAs for the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the special trust fund.

We recommended and Management already cause the reversion of the unspent balance of the LDRRM Fund of more than five years to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and thereafter, appropriate it for other social services through legislative enactment.
We recommended also that the Local Chief Executive instruct the DRRM Officer to revise its LDRRMIP as well as to revise AIP to include PPAs for the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the special trust fund.

We recommended that Management require various departments/offices of the City to observe the schedule in submitting GAD Planning and Budget pursuant to Item C.5 of the PCW – DILG – DBM – NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 so that timely review, approval, and submission to concerned Offices can be made.

We also recommended that the City Mayor reconstitute immediately Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee (EXECOM) and Technical Working Group (TWG) pursuant to Item 4.A.6 of PCW – DILG – DBM – NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 so that administrative and logistical services and other GAD related activities can be effectively carried out.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the City Planning and Development Office to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other information and to coordinate with the City GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and the Local DILG Field Officer on the generation and processing of the data in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01;
Ensure the effective implementation of the City GAD Database that contains systematically gathered and regularly updated information relevant to gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
Ensure that adequate budget is provided for the establishment and maintenance of the GAD Database.

barangays as per
agreed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant reconcile their records and correct the discrepancy of Cash-Local Treasury account totaling P583,925.31 in the books.
Henceforth, regular quarterly reconciliation required under Section 181 of the GAAM, Volume I should be strictly followed to avoid existence or accumulation of discrepancies and ensure timely correction of errors, if needed.

We recommended that City Mayor require all accountable officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unliquidated cash advances specifically those granted December 31, 2011 and below considering that a final demand was already issued pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
Moreover, the City Accountant shall ensure that all advances to Officers and Employees and Special Disbursing Officer are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
The City Mayor direct the Inventory Committee and the CGSO to conduct yearly physical count of its properties and submit a complete Report on Physical Count of PPEs, Investment Property and Biological Assets pursuant to Section 124 of Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to validate the reported balances of the above properties.
The City Accountant and CGSO implement the immediate reconciliation of their records in as much as this deficiency has long been reported in the Annual Audit Report.
Any discrepancies between accounting records and actual inventory report of Breeding Stocks under Biological Assets account  be immediately reconciled so that any unbooked assets pertaining to breeding stocks, plants and trees could be appropriately recognized in the books by the Accounting Office.
The Inventory Committee ensure that the Physical Inventory Report must contain sufficient and actual information on items counted as to complete description, condition, whether serviceable or unserviceable, number of items counted and specify whether there is an overage or shortage.
Management commented thru their City General Services Office (CGSO) that they are in the process of conducting actual physical count of property, plant and equipment based on the subsidiary ledger from Accounting Office, so as to assure and established that the amount of inventory of PPE’s are fairly presented in the financial statement.
The CGSO also commented that the City Accounting should also update their records as to the physical inventory report submitted by the CGSO.
Additionally during exit conference, as far as Biological Assets are concerned, the respective department in-charge will submit the inventory report to the Accounting Office every year for reconciliation.

We recommended that the Office of the General Services shall file a request for disposal of those unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with Section 167 Rule 22 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
During exit conference, the CGSO noted the recommendation to file a request for disposal for those additional identified unserviceable motor vehicles. The Audit Team also commented to follow up the previous request forwarded to the Technical Audit Specialist.

We recommended that the City Engineers Office submit regularly to the Office of the City Accountant the Accomplishment Report on Infrastructure Projects and copies of Certificate of Completion and Acceptance to be used as basis for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the transfer to appropriate PPE – Public Infrastructure accounts.
We further recommended that the City adhere to the provisions of Section 50 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I to enable close monitoring of the projects still in progress and to make necessary adjusting entries to transfer the identified completed projects to avoid overstatement of the ‘Construction in Progress" accounts.
During exit conference, the City Accountant commented that Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) were already made to transfer those completed projects to its appropriate asset account.
Further, an agreement was made with the City Engineer to regularly submit to the City Accountant the Complete List of Completed Projects to be used as a basis to record the transfer of asset as well as to the CGSO for inclusion in its inventory.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to ensure that required documentary requirements are duly attached to the disbursement vouchers before approving any release of fund transfers to various barangays pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects so that the optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained; and
City Accountant and City Treasurer to immediately refund/remit the excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to avoid possible loss or misuse.
During exit conference, Management commented that some of the projects were already completed but were not transferred to the appropriate asset account, others are on-going, for delivery and some are scheduled for bidding this quarter.

We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the utilization of the PCSO Charity Fund in accordance with R.A. No. 1169, as amended by Executive Order No. 357 to become more effective partner of the National Government in the extension of services to the marginal sector in the community.
During exit conference, the City Mayor direct the department head concerned to facilitate the utilization and implementation of the PCSO Charity Fund.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to:
Effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the receipt of PCSO Share;
Strictly adhere to the Chart of Accounts in the recording of the LGU Share from PCSO Charity Fund for better control purposes; and
Ensure that income from all sources are properly appropriated, allotted and obligated only for duly approved programs, projects and activities during the budget year.

We recommended that City of Kabankalan secure first approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to implementation of projects/programs/activities funded out of the lump-sum appropriation pursuant to Supreme Court En Banc Decision G.R. No. 175527 dated December 8, 2008 and reiterated in COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 dated April 22, 2010.
During exit conference, the City Budget Officer reasoned out that in the separate form, the Electrification Program were already itemized. Additionally, for the other three identified programs which were implemented in CY 2019, prior approval from the Sanggunian has already been secured.

We recommended and Management agreed to formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils (LDCs) taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.
We also recommended that City strictly observe the guidelines on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-073 dated April 17, 2012.
Furthermore, we recommended that City Accountant makes the necessary adjusting entries to record the completed projects in its respective asset account totaling P7,837,863.13 in the Trust fund book and correspondingly transfer to the General Fund books of account. The corresponding depreciation thereof must also be recognized.
During exit conference, the City Mayor instructed the LDRRM Officer to follow and comply with the audit finding and recommendation of the audit team. The LDRRM Officer commented that they are in the process of updating the comprehensive LDRRM Plan. Also, the improper charging against 5% LDRRM Fund were discontinued.
Journal Entry Vouchers were already made to record the completed projects as well as its transfer to the General Fund book.

We recommended and Management agreed to formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils (LDCs) taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.
We also recommended that City strictly observe the guidelines on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-073 dated April 17, 2012.
Furthermore, we recommended that City Accountant makes the necessary adjusting entries to record the completed projects in its respective asset account totaling P7,837,863.13 in the Trust fund book and correspondingly transfer to the General Fund books of account. The corresponding depreciation thereof must also be recognized.
During exit conference, the City Mayor instructed the LDRRM Officer to follow and comply with the audit finding and recommendation of the audit team. The LDRRM Officer commented that they are in the process of updating the comprehensive LDRRM Plan. Also, the improper charging against 5% LDRRM Fund were discontinued.
Journal Entry Vouchers were already made to record the completed projects as well as its transfer to the General Fund book.

The City will established a comprehensive plan, so that the allotted amount for such fund will be utilized and implemented.
Projects were implemented in the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:  
The City Mayor direct the conduct of proper investigation on all Accountable Officers at the City Treasurer’s to determine all persons responsible and liable on the irregularities that occurred therein, submit a report thereon and file appropriate charges, if necessary, as soon as possible;  

The City Treasurer install a strong internal control measures to ensure that issuance of Official Receipt could not be altered by any Accountable Officer and the access to the computerized system must be assigned only to authorized employees of the Office and monitor activities of collectors to ensure that they are adhering to existing rules and regulations; and  

Moreover, she must not allow the collection function to employees who are not duly designated as collectors and not properly bonded pursuant to Section 101 of PD 1445 and Sec. 305(f) RA No. 7160.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management adhere strictly to the General Provisions of the GAA and ensure that the employees’ salaries are protected from further deductions which will reduce their net take home pay below the minimum amount.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
The City Mayor demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 for those granted before December 31, 2011;
The City Accountant require the accountable officers to strictly comply with the guidelines provided in Items 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 in the liquidation of their cash advances; and
The City Accountant monitor the liquidation and demand settlement if no liquidation was submitted within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommended that payments made out of the LDRRM Fund should be supported with complete documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the validity of the claims against the fund.

Recommendation:
We recommendedthat the City Accountant initiate action for the liquidation of the outstanding fund transfer through sending demand letters to the NGOs/POs concerned and demanding return of the fund if found not utilized or has been used not in accordance with the  Memorandum of Agreement; and
We recommended further that the City Government follow strictly Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 on the granting of fund transfer to NGOs/POs.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City submit the 2017 GAD accomplishment report of all offices concerned pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-01 dated April 5, 2004 of the DBM, NEDA and PCW; and
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person ensure that the GAD Plan includes programs and projects that promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights and promote economic empowerment.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon within the prescribed deadline pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Training Handbook on Property & Supply Management System, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and General Services Office records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management review the unutilized balances of the LDRRMF as of December 31, 2017, to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disaster and climate change impacts.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Accountant further analyze and evaluate unpaid obligations that remained outstanding for more than two years totaling P2,929,664.58 and effect its reversion to the unappropriated surplus, pursuant to  Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
Likewise, we recommended that the City Accountant should also determine the reason for the P368,145.43 negative balance included in the account “Accounts Payable.”  Adjusting entries should subsequently be prepared, if necessary.

Recommendation:
We recommended for management to strictly cause the remittance in full of the amount withheld to the BIR within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding accrued. This practice shall be constantly enforced.

Recommendation:
We recommended that programs/projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund  be maximized considering that these are priority projects that provide for  the immediate needs of the constituents socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

We recommended that the city require the accountable officers concerned to immediately submit liquidation documents to settle their overdue cash advances and abide with the regulations set forth under Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency intensify its collection and develop an effective method of revenue collections to ensure that programs/projects for the constituents are implemented.

We recommended that the City Accountant further analyze and evaluate unpaid obligations that remained outstanding for more than two years totaling P1,617,195.52 and effect its reversion to the unappropriated surplus, pursuant to Section 98 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City issue demand letters to individual/person to enforce payment of their outstanding loans. Also, require the NGOs/POs to submit financial and physical status reports and accomplishment report on the utilization of financial assistance granted.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report
Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor through the CSWD ensure that Livelihood Associations granted financial assistance are qualified based on the SEA-K guidelines and the release of assistance must be supported with MOA and other essential supporting documents; and

The CSWD ascertain that all associations granted with capital assistance pay their monthly dues to the City so that similar financial assistance could be granted to other livelihood associations so poor families will be given equal opportunities to improve their financial status.

We recommended that the City Accountant immediately submit disbursement vouchers, official receipts and supporting documents due for submission to the Audit Team.

Henceforth, she must strictly adhere to the deadline of submission of aforementioned documents pursuant to Section 7.2.1 a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 100 and 107 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS pursuant Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to have an assurance of the validity of the Cash in Bank balance as of December 31, 2016.

The City Accounting and City Treasurer’s Office reconcile also their records on Cash in Bank-LC-Time Deposit to ensure the reliability of the balances recorded in the books.

The City Accountant furnish the Audit Team a copy of the BRS monthly and on time so the team could conduct timely verification of the report. Further, a written request should be made to the bank monthly to expedite release of the bank statements so that the preparation of the BRS could made on time.

We recommended that the City direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs are fairly presented in the financial statements.

Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and GSO records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City submit the 2016 GAD accomplishment report to all offices concerned pursuant to JC No. 2004-01 dated April 5, 2004 of the DBM, NEDA, and PCW.

The GAD Focal Person ensure that the GAD Plan includes programs and projects that promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights and promote economic empowerment.

We recommended that the City Mayor adhere to the provisions of RA 10121 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 by establishing an LDRRMO pursuant to the manpower requirement under 4.2 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC so that the City could plan and implement projects and programs that will address pre-disaster activities to avoid the adverse impacts of hazards.
